Empires & Puzzles Costumed Diaochan Hero Review & Strategy Guide

She broke the rules. With minions that can't be stopped, removed, or disabled, Costumed Diaochan became an instant nightmare. But with new mega-fiends and ninja counters, is her reign of terror finally over?
Costumed Diaochan: The Unstoppable Flower Beauty

When Costumed Diaochan was released in May 2024, she didn't just shift the meta; she broke it. By introducing the "Unstoppable Minions" mechanic, she fundamentally altered how the game deals with summons. No longer could you rely on Grimble or Bera to wipe the board. This "Flower Beauty" brought massive stats, a beefier minion, and a passive that made players question the sanity of the developers. Even now, amidst rapid power creep, she remains a formidable force.

The costume transforms Diaochan from a court sorceress into a floral goddess. The art is vibrant, dominated by soft pinks and purples, with a more whimsical and ethereal vibe compared to the sharper, more serious tone of the original. The "Flower Beauty" moniker is well-earned, though don't let the delicate petals fool you—this is one of the most resilient and annoying heroes in the game's history.

With a base power of 1105 (and significantly higher with Limit Breaks), Costumed Diaochan sits comfortably in the "Modern Era" tier of stats. While newer heroes from late 2025/2026 are pushing 1260+ power, Diaochan's stats are still robust enough to serve as a tank or a sturdy flank. Her defense (nearly 1100 base) and massive health pool (over 2100 base) allow her to absorb tremendous punishment, making her 33% minions incredibly difficult to cut through.
This skill is a masterclass in utility and sustain.
33% heal to all at Fast speed. It's slightly less than the base version (35%), but the trade-off is negligible given her higher base HP.

Unlike the paper-thin minions of the base version, these minions inherit 33% HP. On a fully maxed Diaochan, that's a minion with nearly 1000 HP. That is essentially an extra hero's worth of health spread across your team.
The buff stealing mechanic remains, turning enemy buffs into your own. With the minions being harder to kill, they stick around longer, stealing more buffs over time.
Fast (8 tiles). With the Kingdom family bonus (mana per minion), she can often charge even faster after the first cast.
She heals 33% (~800-900 HP) and adds a 33% minion (~800-900 HP). That is a ~1600-1800 HP swing for herself and neighbors at Fast speed. That is absurd value.
These minions are tanks. Requiring ~1000 damage to remove just one minion means enemies must waste special skills just to clear the "meat shield" before they can touch your HP.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
This is where Costumed Diaochan goes from "Great" to "Broken".
The mana generation on minion summon is dangerous. Once Diaochan gets going, she fuels her own mana bar with every cast, creating a snowball effect where she heals and summons faster and faster.
Grimble, Topaz, Captain of Diamonds? Useless. They do damage, but the minions stay.
Bera or Kvasir cannot stop her from summoning.

Lunar heroes (like Laohu) who passively disable minion effects have no power here. Her minions will continue to steal buffs and attack no matter what.
This passive removes 90% of the counter-play that existed for minion summoners.
. A perfect fit. Unlike the base version's useless Attack Up, this boosts her survivability, ensuring she stays alive to keep the minion factory running.
Upon release, Costumed Diaochan was declared "Broken" by the community. She dominated defenses, creating "Time Out" scenarios simply because enemies couldn't kill her minions fast enough.

While her stats have been surpassed by the very latest 2026 heroes, her "Unstoppable" mechanic prevents her from becoming obsolete. Raw damage can eventually break her minions, and "Mega Fiends" or specific "Minion Health Halving" passives (like on new Ninjas) have been introduced specifically to counter her.

She remains one of the best healers in the game. Her ability to force enemies to chew through thousands of HP in minions, while being immune to standard counters, keeps her relevant in War Defense and PvP offense.
The costume offers massive stat increases and the superior "Defense Up" Aether power.
33% HP (Costume) vs 16% HP (Base). The costume's minions are twice as strong.
The "Unstoppable" passive makes the Costume infinitely superior. The Base version is vulnerable to every minion counter; the Costume ignores them.
Cleric (Costume) allows her to resist mana control, ensuring she fires. Sorcerer (Base) is less consistent for a healer.
The Costume is the definitive version. It is an upgrade in every conceivable metric.
With her role as a tank/flank and the %-based nature of her minions, HP and Defense are key.
Maximizes Minion HP (since it's 33% of her total HP). A higher HP pool makes her minions harder to kill, which is her main strength.
Slightly lower defense stat for Diaochan herself.
Makes Diaochan herself tougher against direct damage.
path is ideal. Since her minions are essentially "extra health" that can't be dispelled, making them as large as possible (by boosting her max HP) gives the most value to your team.
Her minions cannot be mass-removed or blocked by standard counters.
High healing + Giant Minions = A team that refuses to die.
Constant stealing of buffs disrupts the enemy's rhythm.
Doing all this at Fast mana is terrifying.

She has no cleanse. Fiends block her healing, and since she relies on raw HP recovery, they can shut her down (though they don't remove her minions, they can displace them if the minion slot is full).
Newer mechanics like Mega Fiends or heroes with specific "Half Minion HP" passives are the only true checks to her power.
She deals no direct damage, relying on her team to win the fight while she stalls.
An absolute staple at Tank or Flank. She forces attackers to bring specific high-damage counters or risk timing out.
Excellent for keeping attackers alive against heavy hitters.
Her minions provide a safety net that makes auto-farming safer.
Costumed Diaochan thrives in stall teams or paired with other "annoying" heroes.
. A mix of healing, ailment blocking, and minions that is a nightmare to chew through.
. Overwhelming the enemy with minions while Queen Guowang punishes them for using specials.
, or heavy fiend summoners can displace her minions or block her healing.
The only mechanic that truly "trumps" her unstoppable minions by replacing them.
Since you can't dispel the minions, you just have to hit them. Hard.
If you have this costume, max it immediately. It will carry you through almost every aspect of the game. It is a "cheat code" for difficult content.

She is still a war defense queen. 2LB is highly recommended for the stat boost and the Defense Aether power. Be aware of the new Ninja heroes with anti-minion passives, but don't let that stop you from using her. She forces the enemy to burn a premium flag.
"Is this a joke? Why such a massive costume bonus?? ... Over than 1100 POW is just a big joke…"
- Community reaction to her release stats.
"This is beyond broken hero now. Usually i’m not against OP heroes... but this is serious problem."
- A common sentiment regarding her "Unstoppable" passive.
"Just a quick observation… I was just browsing the top raid defenses and it seemed to me that costumed Diaochan is prevalent in most of the teams currently in top 100… what a surprise"

Costumed Diaochan is a testament to the power of "breaking the rules." By ignoring the established counters of the minion meta, she carved out a throne that she sits on comfortably to this day. While the winds of power creep are always blowing, her unique mechanics ensure she remains a "Flower Beauty" with thorns that will prick players for years to come.
An absolute must-have. One of the strongest defensive healers in the game's history.
Is Costumed Diaochan broken? A review of the 'Unstoppable' healer who changed the meta with her uncounterable minions and massive stats.
